comment: The classic driving routes. stops = ordered curated-attraction ids (validated). dayBreaks = stop indexes that BEGIN a new day when bulk-adding to a trip. Geometry generated by scripts/dev/make-route-geojson.mjs (OSRM) into routes/<id>.geojson.

routes.name: Golden Circle
routes.seasonNote: Drivable year-round on paved roads — icy patches in winter.
routes.description: THE classic day loop: the rift valley where Iceland's parliament was born, an erupting geyser, and the golden waterfall — with hot-spring soaks and greenhouse tomato soup on the way.

routes.name: South Coast
routes.seasonNote: Year-round and spectacular in winter — but wind and ice demand respect.
routes.description: Waterfalls you walk behind, a glacier tongue, puffin cliffs and the black beach — Iceland's greatest-hits corridor along Route 1 to Vík and the moss canyons beyond.

routes.name: Ring Road (Route 1)
routes.seasonNote: Jun–Sep is straightforward; winter circuits are possible but storms close sections — build in buffer days.
routes.description: The whole country in one loop: south-coast waterfalls, the glacier lagoon, East Fjord villages, Mývatn's volcanic wonderland, and the north's fjord towns. A week is the honest minimum.

routes.name: Snæfellsnes Peninsula
routes.seasonNote: 'Iceland in miniature' — doable as a long day, better as two. Fine in winter with a careful forecast check.
routes.description: Seal beaches, a black church, cliff walks, a lava-tube cave, the glacier volcano of Jules Verne — and Kirkjufell. All of Iceland's textures on one peninsula.

routes.name: Diamond Circle
routes.seasonNote: Full loop ~May–Oct; in winter Dettifoss access often closes while the Mývatn area stays reachable.
routes.description: The north's answer to the Golden Circle: the gods' waterfall, whale watching in Húsavík, the echoing horseshoe of Ásbyrgi, Europe's mightiest waterfall, and Mývatn's boiling earth.

routes.name: Reykjanes Peninsula
routes.seasonNote: Year-round — and perfect for a first or last day near the airport. Eruption-era closures possible; check safetravel.is.
routes.description: A UNESCO geopark of steaming mud pools, fresh lava fields, a bridge between continents and the Blue Lagoon — all within an hour of Keflavík airport.

routes.name: Westfjords Way
routes.seasonNote: Mountain passes and gravel — realistically Jun–Sep. The reward: Iceland with the crowds deleted.
routes.description: The remote northwest: witchcraft lore in Hólmavík, the fjord capital Ísafjörður, the bridal-veil giant Dynjandi, red sands, and millions of seabirds at Europe's westernmost cliffs.
